channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
item.description: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.
item.content:encoded: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.
item.dc:creator: Scott Tong

item.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
item.description: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.
item.content:encoded:
Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

(Image credit: Suy Se)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: "Lives will be lost": How the U.K.'s aid cuts may affect parts of Africa
item.description: In March, the UK announced it would trim its global aid budget and set new priorities in 2027. This has some countries and organizations worried that on top of the US aid cuts, this could be unsurvivable.
item.content:encoded:
In March, the UK announced it would trim its global aid budget and set new priorities in 2027. This has some countries and organizations worried that on top of the US aid cuts, this could be unsurvivable.

(Image credit: Luis Tato/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: This fringe actor finds the spotlight in Israel's most provocative film post-Oct. 7
item.description: Israeli fringe actor in the spotlight for anti-war film
item.content:encoded:
It landed him a best actor nomination at Israel's academy award ceremony last year. He didn't win.'/>

Israeli fringe actor in the spotlight for anti-war film

(Image credit: Ofir Berman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Trump makes his case for war with Iran, saying the conflict is 'nearing completion'
item.description: Trump used a prime-time address to outline his objectives for the war and discuss the military's progress to date. He also repeated an earlier claim that fighting could end in two to three weeks.
item.content:encoded:
Trump used a prime-time address to outline his objectives for the war and discuss the military's progress to date. He also repeated an earlier claim that fighting could end in two to three weeks.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: After Israel's invasion, many in southern Lebanon worry they'll never go home
item.description: Israel says it's creating a "security zone" inside Lebanon, where homes will be demolished, and residents won't be allowed to return home until people in northern Israel feel safe.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it's creating a "security zone" inside Lebanon, where homes will be demolished, and residents won't be allowed to return home until people in northern Israel feel safe.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Greetings from London, where a big COVID memorial holds vigil over the halls of power
item.description: Britain's 1,640-foot-long National Covid Memorial Wall stretches along the River Thames, with over a quarter million hearts representing the lives lost during the pandemic — across from Parliament.
item.content:encoded: Britain's 1,640-foot-long National Covid Memorial Wall stretches along the River Thames, with over a quarter million hearts representing the lives lost during the pandemic — across from Parliament.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Ni

item.title: Trump to address nation on Iran war. And, SCOTUS considers birthright citizenship
item.description: Trump is set to address the nation after saying the U.S. will withdraw from Iran in 2-3 weeks. And, the president will be at the Supreme Court today as arguments on birthright citizenship begin.
item.content:encoded:
Trump is set to address the nation after saying the U.S. will withdraw from Iran in 2-3 weeks. And, the president will be at the Supreme Court today as arguments on birthright citizenship begin.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon as Trump is set to deliver a speech on the Iran war
item.description: As Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon, the White House says President Trump will provide "an important update" to the nation on the Iran war.
item.content:encoded:
As Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon, the White House says President Trump will provide "an important update" to the nation on the Iran war.

(Image credit: Bilal Hussein)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
